categories.name: أَلْفَاظٌ تَصِفُ مَرَاحِلَ الْعُمْرِ أَوِ الْوَقْتِ
categories.nameEn: Words describing stages of human life or periods of time

items.verse: يَـٰيَحْيَىٰ خُذِ ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ بِقُوَّةٍۢ ۖ وَءَاتَيْنَـٰهُ ٱلْحُكْمَ صَبِيًّۭا
items.verseEn: [Allāh said], "O John, take the Scripture [i.e., adhere to it] with determination." And We gave him judgement [while yet] a boy

items.verse: وَيُكَلِّمُ ٱلنَّاسَ فِى ٱلْمَهْدِ وَكَهْلًۭا وَمِنَ ٱلصَّـٰلِحِينَ
items.verseEn: He will speak to the people in the cradle and in maturity and will be of the righteous."

items.verse: قَالُوا۟ يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلْعَزِيزُ إِنَّ لَهُۥٓ أَبًۭا شَيْخًۭا كَبِيرًۭا فَخُذْ أَحَدَنَا مَكَانَهُۥٓ ۖ إِنَّا نَرَىٰكَ مِنَ ٱلْمُحْسِنِينَ
items.verseEn: They said, "O ʿAzeez, indeed he has a father [who is] an old man, so take one of us in place of him. Indeed, we see you as a doer of good."

items.verse: وَوَصَّيْنَا ٱلْإِنسَـٰنَ بِوَٰلِدَيْهِ إِحْسَـٰنًا ۖ حَمَلَتْهُ أُمُّهُۥ كُرْهًۭا وَوَضَعَتْهُ كُرْهًۭا ۖ وَحَمْلُهُۥ وَفِصَـٰلُهُۥ ثَلَـٰثُونَ شَهْرًا ۚ حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا بَلَغَ أَشُدَّهُۥ وَبَلَغَ أَرْبَعِينَ سَنَةًۭ قَالَ رَبِّ أَوْزِعْنِىٓ أَنْ أَشْكُرَ نِعْمَتَكَ ٱلَّتِىٓ أَنْعَمْتَ عَلَىَّ وَعَلَىٰ وَٰلِدَىَّ وَأَنْ أَعْمَلَ صَـٰلِحًۭا تَرْضَىٰهُ وَأَصْلِحْ لِى فِى ذُرِّيَّتِىٓ ۖ إِنِّى تُبْتُ إِلَيْكَ وَإِنِّى مِنَ ٱلْمُسْلِمِينَ
items.verseEn: And We have enjoined upon man, to his parents, good treatment. His mother carried him with hardship and gave birth to him with hardship, and his gestation and weaning [period] is thirty months. [He grows] until, when he reaches maturity and reaches [the age of] forty years, he says, "My Lord, enable me to be grateful for Your favor which You have bestowed upon me and upon my parents and to work righteousness of which You will approve and make righteous for me my offspring. Indeed, I have repented to You, and indeed, I am of the Muslims."

categories.name: صِفَاتٌ وَقَعَتْ خَبَرًا لِـ 'كَانَ'
categories.nameEn: Adjectives that are the predicate (khabar) of 'kāna' (was)

items.verse: وَقُلْ جَآءَ ٱلْحَقُّ وَزَهَقَ ٱلْبَـٰطِلُ ۚ إِنَّ ٱلْبَـٰطِلَ كَانَ زَهُوقًۭا
items.verseEn: And say, "Truth has come, and falsehood has departed. Indeed is falsehood, [by nature], ever bound to depart."

items.verse: أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ ٱلَّذِينَ يَدْعُونَ يَبْتَغُونَ إِلَىٰ رَبِّهِمُ ٱلْوَسِيلَةَ أَيُّهُمْ أَقْرَبُ وَيَرْجُونَ رَحْمَتَهُۥ وَيَخَافُونَ عَذَابَهُۥٓ ۚ إِنَّ عَذَابَ رَبِّكَ كَانَ مَحْذُورًۭا
items.verseEn: Those whom they invoke seek means of access to their Lord, [striving as to] which of them would be nearest, and they hope for His mercy and fear His punishment. Indeed, the punishment of your Lord is ever feared.

items.verse: وَإِن مِّنكُمْ إِلَّا وَارِدُهَا ۚ كَانَ عَلَىٰ رَبِّكَ حَتْمًۭا مَّقْضِيًّۭا
items.verseEn: And there is none of you except he will come to it. This is upon your Lord an inevitability decreed.

items.verse: كُلُّ ذَٰلِكَ كَانَ سَيِّئُهُۥ عِندَ رَبِّكَ مَكْرُوهًۭا
items.verseEn: All that [i.e., the aforementioned] - its evil is ever, in the sight of your Lord, detested.

categories.name: الْحُرُوفُ الْمُقَطَّعَةُ
categories.nameEn: Disjoined letters (Al-Muqatta'at) found at the beginning of some surahs

items.verseEn: Alif, Lām, Rā. These are the verses of the wise Book.

categories.name: أَلْفَاظٌ لِلْإِدْرَاكِ مِنْ جُذُورٍ غَيْرِ شَائِعَةٍ
categories.nameEn: Words for perception from less common roots

items.verse: وَٱقْتَرَبَ ٱلْوَعْدُ ٱلْحَقُّ فَإِذَا هِىَ شَـٰخِصَةٌ أَبْصَـٰرُ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ يَـٰوَيْلَنَا قَدْ كُنَّا فِى غَفْلَةٍۢ مِّنْ هَـٰذَا بَلْ كُنَّا ظَـٰلِمِينَ
items.verseEn: And [when] the true promise [i.e., the resurrection] has approached; then suddenly the eyes of those who disbelieved will be staring [in horror, while they say], "O woe to us; we had been unmindful of this; rather, we were wrongdoers."
And he will look and see him in the midst of the Hellfire.

items.en: for those who perceive by signs
items.verseEn: Indeed in that are signs for those who discern.

items.verse: إِلَّا مَنِ ٱسْتَرَقَ ٱلسَّمْعَ فَأَتْبَعَهُۥ شِهَابٌۭ مُّبِينٌۭ
items.verseEn: Except one who steals a hearing and is pursued by a clear burning flame.

harf.hint: A divine assurance that is always kept.
harf.arabicVerse: يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلنَّاسُ إِنَّ وَعْدَ ٱللَّهِ حَقٌّۭ ۖ فَلَا تَغُرَّنَّكُمُ ٱلْحَيَوٰةُ ٱلدُّنْيَا ۖ وَلَا يَغُرَّنَّكُم بِٱللَّهِ ٱلْغَرُورُ
harf.verse: 35:5 — O mankind, indeed the promise of Allāh is truth, so let not the worldly life delude you and be not deceived about Allāh by the Deceiver [i.e., Satan].

deduction.title: The Testimony of the Flames
deduction.intro: A community faced a stark choice from their tyrannical ruler: renounce their newfound faith or face a terrible fate. Their unwavering conviction led to an event so momentous that Allah mentions it in the Quran, a testament to their sacrifice and a warning to all oppressors. Who were these believers?
clues: We were a community that came to believe in the One True God, turning away from the worship of our ruler.
Our newfound faith enraged the powerful leader who had once commanded our absolute devotion.
We were given an ultimatum: abandon our belief in Allah or face a horrific punishment of their design.
To carry out their threat, they dug a great trench and kindled a massive fire within it.
While our persecutors sat and watched, they cast us into the blazing pit for all to see.
Our only 'crime' in their eyes was that we believed in Allah, the Exalted in Might, the Praiseworthy.

identity.label: Who Are We?
options: The People of the Elephant
The People of 'Ad
The People of the Ditch
The Companions of the Sabbath
The People of Rass

trial.label: What Was Our Trial?
options: Being drowned in a great flood
Being pelted with stones of baked clay
Being transformed into apes
Being burned alive for our faith
Being struck by a mighty earthquake
trial.answer: Being burned alive for our faith

location.label: What Was the Key Element?
options: A giant ark
A staff that turned into a serpent
A she-camel sent as a sign
A valley of ants
A trench filled with fire

outcome.label: What Was Our Ultimate Outcome?
options: We were saved and made into a great nation
We repented and our punishment was lifted
We attained Paradise for our patience and faith
We were resurrected after one hundred years
We slept for over 300 years in a cave
outcome.answer: We attained Paradise for our patience and faith

deduction.arabic: إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ لَهُمْ جَنَّـٰتٌۭ تَجْرِى مِن تَحْتِهَا ٱلْأَنْهَـٰرُ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ ٱلْفَوْزُ ٱلْكَبِيرُ
deduction.verse: Indeed, those who have believed and done righteous deeds will have gardens beneath which rivers flow. That is the great attainment. (85:11)

scramble.hint: This verse concludes a surah known for its beautiful refrain, praising the attributes of Allah.
scramble.arabic: تَبَـٰرَكَ ٱسْمُ رَبِّكَ ذِى ٱلْجَلَـٰلِ وَٱلْإِكْرَامِ
scramble.verseEn: Blessed is the name of your Lord, Owner of Majesty and Honor.
